vulnerability.reference: Low-resource language communities lack alternative tools, forcing reliance on degraded LLM outputs.
vulnerability.generated: Low-resource languages lack tokenizer training data, leaving them defenseless against distribution-distorting noncanonical token encodings.

impact.reference: Misinformation spreads in low-resource language communities due to plausible but corrupted text.
impact.generated: Speakers receive linguistically meaningless, garbled text, rendering generation tools unusable for their languages.

dose_response.reference: Tokenization mismatch distorts token probabilities, which in multilingual text generation exposes low-resource language speakers to linguistically meaningless segments, leading to misinformation spread and economic exclusion.
dose_response.generated: Tokenization mismatch between training and inference induces noncanonical encodings that, through low-resource language speakers using LLM text generators, exploit defenselessness from lacking tokenizer training data, leading to garbled meaningless text and unusable tools.
